title: jupunba idiopoda
intro_study: According to "Journal of ethnopharmacology", jupunba idiopoda (also known as Vitex megapotamica) has antinociceptive and antidepressant-like effects, inducing opioid-dependent antinociception and antidepressant-like effects without anti-inflammatory activity.
side_effects: Jupunba idiopoda can have side effects if used improperly, such as severe allergic reactions, including hives, itching, and difficulty breathing. Overuse of the plant's extracts can lead to stomach problems, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Long-term use of jupunba idiopoda without proper medical guidance can also cause liver and kidney damage, as the plant's toxins can accumulate in the body over time. Furthermore, improper preparation or dosage of the plant's extracts can cause cardiac issues, including irregular heartbeat and palpitations.
